sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: MOUSE(row=17, col=20).
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 131, level 2.
Valid actions right now: MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: VERIFIED(step 2-7): Clicking red block (4x3, ~40px) rotates perimeter blocks clockwise by 1 position. Clicking green block or colored blocks does nothing. Green strip at col 0 shrinks 5px per rotation.
- Object taxonomy: Red block (40px, left edge)=rotation trigger. Light green strip (col 0)=progress bar. Light green block (40px, right edge)=fixed decoration. Colored 2x2/4x4 blocks rotate on perimeter. Yellow 2x2 markers=fixed indicators. Black 2x2 blocks=HUD.
- HUD map: Left green strip (col 0) shrinks 5px per rotation from 64px initial. Top gray area has black blocks. Charcoal background.
- Win pattern: Rotate until green progress bar depletes. Level completes when strip reaches 0.
- Level log: Level 1: Click red block repeatedly (8 rotations) until green strip at col 0 shrinks from 64px to 0. Score=1.
- Cross-level notes: Levels have different layouts but same rotation mechanic via red block trigger.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 123 actions; a strong score needs about 38 or fewer. You are 3.2x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 61 actions returned the board to a state already seen 61 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 61 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

Key insight: I've been rotating blindly without understanding the completion condition. The level has 3 rings with colored blocks, and 3 triggers:
- (17,20): rotates ALL rings, strip -1px
- (25,13): rotates middle ring only
- (34,13): rotates bottom ring only
The completion condition might be that the blocks need to be in a specific configuration. Let me think about what that configuration might be.

- Top ring: blocks at rows 17, 20, 23 with various colors
- Middle ring: blocks at row 26
- Bottom ring: blocks at rows 29, 32, 35, 38, 41, 44

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game auto-reset, now at step 131. Strip was 64, now should be 63 after 1 rotation.
# Need to understand completion condition better.

# Check current strip
seg = current_frame.segmentation
green_strip = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] > 10]
if green_strip:
    b = green_strip[0]['boundary']
    strip_len = max(p[0] for p in b) - min(p[0] for p in b) + 1
    print(f"Strip: {strip_len}px")

# Get colored blocks by row to understand the pattern
from collections import defaultdict
blocks_by_row = defaultdict(list)
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 4 and n['color'] not in ['Y', 'N', 'B', 'G', 'c']:
        b = n['boundary']
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b)//4
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b)//4
        blocks_by_row[cr].append((cc, n['color']))

print("Blocks by row:")
for row in sorted(blocks_by_row.keys()):
    print(f"  {row}: {sorted(blocks_by_row[row])}")

# Check if there are any new indicators - look for special colors
special = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] in ['M', 'P', 'O', 'r'] and n['pixels'] <= 10]
print(f"Special small objects: {len(special)}")

# Try rotating 10 times with (17,20) and check for completion
for i in range(10):
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 17, 'col': 20}])
    if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('done'):
        print(f"Level completed after {i+1} rotations!")
        break
    if last_action_result.get('game_over'):
        print(f"Game over after {i+1} rotations!")
        break
else:
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    green_strip = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] > 10]
    if green_strip:
        b = green_strip[0]['boundary']
        strip_len = max(p[0] for p in b) - min(p[0] for p in b) + 1
        print(f"After 10 more rots: strip={strip_len}px, not complete")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": "VERIFIED: (17,20) rotates ALL rings, strip -1px. (25,13) rotates middle ring only. (34,13) rotates bottom ring only.", "object_taxonomy": "3 rings with colored blocks, 3 yellow markers, green strip progress bar, red trigger blocks", "hud_map": "Left green strip shrinks 1px/rotation from 64px", "win_pattern": "UNKNOWN: strip=0 alone not sufficient; need correct block config too", "level_log": "Level 1: 8 rots completed. Level 2: 61 rots, game over at step 129. Auto-reset, now step 131.", "cross_level_notes": "Level 2 has 3 triggers with different scopes; completion requires more than just strip depletion"}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Auto-reset occurred, strip at 63px after 1 rotation. Testing if strip=0 completes level.", "goal_model": "Find target block configuration that completes level", "recent_findings": ["Game auto-reset after game over", "Step count continues after reset"], "open_questions": ["What is the target block configuration?", "Is there a step limit?"], "current_plan": "Rotate with (17,20) and check for completion at each step", "strategy_log": ["Blind rotation 61 times failed, game over at step 129"], "failed_probes": ["61 rots with (17,20) - game over without completion"]}}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game auto-reset, now at step 131. Strip was 64, now should be 63 after 1 rotation.
# Need to understand completion condition better.

# Check current strip
seg = current_frame.segmentation
green_strip = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] > 10]
if green_strip:
    b = green_strip[0]['boundary']
    strip_len = max(p[0] for p in b) - min(p[0] for p in b) + 1
    print(f"Strip: {strip_len}px")

# Get colored blocks by row to understand the pattern
from collections import defaultdict
blocks_by_row = defaultdict(list)
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 4 and n['color'] not in ['Y', 'N', 'B', 'G', 'c']:
        b = n['boundary']
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b)//4
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b)//4
        blocks_by_row[cr].append((cc, n['color']))

print("Blocks by row:")
for row in sorted(blocks_by_row.keys()):
    print(f"  {row}: {sorted(blocks_by_row[row])}")

# Check if there are any new indicators - look for special colors
special = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] in ['M', 'P', 'O', 'r'] and n['pixels'] <= 10]
print(f"Special small objects: {len(special)}")

# Try rotating 10 times with (17,20) and check for completion
for i in range(10):
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 17, 'col': 20}])
    if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('done'):
        print(f"Level completed after {i+1} rotations!")
        break
    if last_action_result.get('game_over'):
        print(f"Game over after {i+1} rotations!")
        break
else:
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    green_strip = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] > 10]
    if green_strip:
        b = green_strip[0]['boundary']
        strip_len = max(p[0] for p in b) - min(p[0] for p in b) + 1
        print(f"After 10 more rots: strip={strip_len}px, not complete")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>

(34,13) rotates bottom ring only.", "object_taxonomy": "3 rings with colored blocks, 3 yellow markers, green strip progress bar, red trigger blocks", "hud_map": "Left green strip shrinks 1px/rotation from 64px", "win_pattern": "UNKNOWN: strip=0 alone not sufficient; need correct block config too", "level_log": "Level 1: 8 rots completed. Level 2: 61 rots, game over at step 129. Auto-reset, now step 131.", "cross_level_notes": "Level 2 has 3 triggers with different scopes; completion requires more than just strip depletion"}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Auto-reset occurred, strip at 63px after 1 rotation. Testing if strip=0 completes level.", "goal_model": "Find target block configuration that completes level", "recent_findings": ["Game auto-reset after game over", "Step count continues after reset"], "open_questions": ["What is the target block configuration?", "Is there a step limit?"], "current_plan": "Rotate with (17,20) and check for completion at each step", "strategy_log": ["Blind rotation 61 times failed, game over at step 129"], "failed_probes": ["61 rots with (17,20) - game over without completion"]}}}
</parameter>
</function>
